summary: Matrix equality under a constant four rotations has a worst-case lower bound of inspecting all n squared target cells, and the coordinate-mapping reference matches that bound without constructing rotations.

optimality.argument: Against an all-zero source and target, changing any one uninspected target cell to one changes the answer from true to false, so a correct worst-case algorithm must inspect all n squared cells. The reference performs four constant-time mapped comparisons per cell.

replacement_checks.evidence: An adversary can place the sole target mismatch at the last cell inspected; no rotation of an all-zero source can match it.
The reference visits every coordinate once and maintains four rotation possibilities with constant work and constant storage.
Authored cases cover all four orientations, one-cell matrices, symmetry, equal cell counts without equality, and impossible targets.
